description: 'newu' is relative newness: recent creation, acquisition, introduction, or beginning in the present context. A book may be new because it was just made or because it has just reached its owner. Newness says nothing about whether the change is better. 'newai' (fresh) concerns retained recent condition, while 'limu' (young) places a developing life or process near its beginning.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry the voice through the nose into stressed true-mid front 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then continue into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: 'newu' and 'newai' (fresh) share a dental nasal and rounded glide. Their related senses stay audible, but the two-syllable 'newu' closes on rounded 'u' beside the wider final hiatus of 'newai'.

semantic_domains.creation: a work or object recently brought into being
semantic_domains.temporal: recent arrival, acquisition, introduction, or beginning
